name: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 — Safety & Health Regulations for Construction
edition: Current as of 2024 (continuously updated by OSHA rulemaking)
description: Enables OCERP built-in validation rules that check project HSE plans, daily reports, and tender packages for OSHA 29 CFR 1926 mandatory provisions: OSHA 10/30-hour cards, Site-Specific Safety Plan (SSSP), Subpart C General Safety, Subpart E PPE, Subpart M Fall Protection, Subpart P Excavations, Subpart Q Concrete & Masonry, Subpart R Steel Erection, Subpart S Underground Construction, Subpart X Stairways & Ladders. Several states (CA Cal/OSHA, MI MIOSHA, WA DOSH, etc.) operate their own approved plans — these rules flag where state plan deltas may apply.
issuer: U.S. Department of Labor — Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
